services.name: pub.alert.notifier:create
services.description: WmPublic. Creates a notifier.

input_parameters.name: name
input_parameters.description: String. Name of the notifier.

input_parameters.name: enabled
input_parameters.description: Boolean. Optional. Specifies whether the notifier is enabled or not. Set to: true to enable the notifier. false to disable the notifier. This is the default. If a notifier is disabled, alerts are not fetched for the notifier.

input_parameters.name: consumptionMode
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. Specifies whether notifications are created for all alerts or only the latest alerts. Set to: earliest to get notifications for all alerts in the system. This is the default. latest to get notifications only for the alerts emitted after the notifier is created.

input_parameters.name: autoMark
input_parameters.description: Boolean. Optional. Specifies whether notifications are automatically marked as read or a user must do it manually. Set to: true to automatically mark a notification as read after the service specified in the notifier settings is invoked. This is the default. false to manually dismiss a notification from Integration Server Administrator, which marks the notification as read.

input_parameters.name: channelIds
input_parameters.description: Object[]. Identifies the channels for which you want to receive notifications. You can specify either an individual channel identifier or a list of identifiers. For example, channelIds[0] as 2 for "Server Error" or channelIds[1] as 10 for "Threat Protection" or channelIds[0] as 2 and channelIds[1] as 1 for both.

input_parameters.name: intervalInSeconds
input_parameters.description: Short. Time in seconds after which the system task created for the notifier should check for unread alerts.

input_parameters.name: severityCutoff
input_parameters.description: String. The severity to consider for fetching alerts. For example, if you set this value as Critical, you will receive notifications only for critical events. If you set this value as Error, you will receive notifications for critical and error events. Set the parameter to one of the following values: Critical. This is the default. Error Information Warning

input_parameters.name: notifyInstantly
input_parameters.description: Boolean. Optional. Specifies whether or not notifications are generated as soon as an alert is raised. Set to: true to instantly generate a notification for an alert. false if an instant notification is not required for an alert. This is the default. Note: If intervalInSeconds is also specified, the notifyInstantly parameter takes precedence.

input_parameters.name: webSocketEnabled
input_parameters.description: Boolean. Optional. Specifies whether or not Integration Server uses a WebSocket connection to send notifications. Set to: true to enable a WebSocket connection to send notifications. false to disable a WebSocket connection. This is the default.

input_parameters.name: webSocketSender
input_parameters.description: Document. Optional. Sends a message on a connected WebSocket session.

input_parameters.name: endPointURL
input_parameters.description: String. The URL of the remote WebSocket server that Integration Server uses to send notifications.

input_parameters.name: serviceInvokeEnabled
input_parameters.description: Boolean. Optional. Specifies whether a service is invoked with the notifier parameters as pipeline variables or not. Set to: true to invoke a service using the notifier parameters. false to disable service invocation for the notifier. This is the default.

input_parameters.name: serviceInvokeSender
input_parameters.description: Document. Optional. Invokes a service for alerts fetched for this notifier.

input_parameters.name: nsName
input_parameters.description: String. The namespace of the service. For example, pub.flow:tracePipeline .

input_parameters.name: smtpEnabled
input_parameters.description: Boolean. Optional. Specifies whether Integration Server uses an SMTP server to send notifications or not. Set to: true to use an SMTP server to send notifications. false if you do not want to use an SMTP server to send notifications. This is the default.

input_parameters.name: smtpSender
input_parameters.description: Document. Optional. Details of the SMTP Server and client used to send the notifications.

input_parameters.name: to
input_parameters.description: String. E-mail address of the recipient for a notification. If you specify multiple addresses, separate them with commas.

input_parameters.name: from
input_parameters.description: String. E-mail address of the sender. If you do not specify this value, Integration Server uses the value specified for the mail.smtp.from JVM property. Furthermore, if a value is not specified for that property, Integration Server uses the default value, user@servername, where the user is the operating system user ID, and servername is the host name of Integration Server.

input_parameters.name: mailhost
input_parameters.description: String. Hostname of the SMTP server.

input_parameters.name: mailhostPort
input_parameters.description: Integer. Optional. The port on which the SMTP server listens to incoming connections.

input_parameters.name: auth
input_parameters.description: Document. Optional. Authorization information that the SMTP service submits to the SMTP server.

input_parameters.name: user
input_parameters.description: String. Username that Integration Server uses to send notifications.

input_parameters.name: pass
input_parameters.description: String. Password associated with the user.

input_parameters.name: secure
input_parameters.description: Document. Parameters specifying the security protocol and trust store information to perform certificate validation, which Integration Server uses to communicate with the SMTP server port.

input_parameters.name: transport​Layer​Security
input_parameters.description: String. Type of the security protocol that Integration Server uses to communicate with the SMTP server port. Set to: none, to use a non-secure mode to communicate with the port on the SMTP server. This is the default. explicit, to use explicit security when communicating with the port on the SMTP server. With explicit security, Integration Server establishes an un-encrypted connection to the e-mail server, and then switches to the secure mode. implicit, to use implicit security to communicate with the port on the SMTP server. With implicit security, Integration Server always establishes an encrypted connection to the e-mail server.

input_parameters.name: truststore​Alias
input_parameters.description: String. Alias for the truststore that contains the list of certificates that Integration Server uses to validate the trust relationship. If you do not specify a truststore alias, the default truststore alias is used.

output_parameters.name: message
output_parameters.description: String. Indicates whether the notifier is successfully created or not.

usage_notes: If you specify the intervalInSeconds parameter while creating a notifier, Integration Server creates a simple interval task that runs in the background and does the tasks specified in the notifier such as invoking a service or sending a mail, and so on.

Prior to Integration Server version 10.15, the pub.alert.notifier:create service included the smtpSender/secure/useJSSE input parameter which specified whether Integration Server created the outbound connection using the Java Secure Socket Extension (JSSE) library. Beginning with Integration Serverversion 10.15, Integration Server establishes all secure outbound connections with JSSE. If you migrated to Integration Server 10.15 and a migrated service invokes pub.alert.notifier:create, the smtpSender/secure/useJSSE parameter still appears in the pipeline. However, Integration Server ignores the value of the smtpSender/secure/useJSSE parameter, proceeding as if useJSSE were set to yes.

services.name: pub.alert.notifier:delete
services.description: WmPublic. Deletes the specified notifier.

input_parameters.name: name
input_parameters.description: String. Name of the notifier that you want to delete.

output_parameters.name: message
output_parameters.description: String. Indicates whether the specified notifier is deleted or not.

usage_notes: The simple interval task scheduled for a notifier executes once, even after the notifier is deleted or disabled.
Each notifier maintains a separate record of read and unread alerts. These records are cleared when the notifier is deleted. However, the alerts themselves are not deleted.

services.name: pub.alert.notifier:disable
services.description: WmPublic. Disables the specified notifier.

input_parameters.name: name
input_parameters.description: String. Name of the notifier that you want to disable.

output_parameters.name: message
output_parameters.description: String. Indicates whether the specified notifier is disabled or not.

usage_notes: The simple interval task scheduled for a notifier executes once, even after the notifier is deleted or disabled.

services.name: pub.alert.notifier:enable
services.description: WmPublic. Enables the specified notifier.

input_parameters.name: name
input_parameters.description: String. Name of the notifier that you want to enable.

output_parameters.name: message
output_parameters.description: String. Indicates whether the specified notifier is enabled or not.

services.name: pub.alert.notifier:list
services.description: WmPublic. Lists all the notifiers in the system.

output_parameters.name: notifiers
output_parameters.description: Document List. The list of notifiers in the system. Each notifier has the following keys:

output_parameters.name: name
output_parameters.description: String. Name of the notifier.

output_parameters.name: enabled
output_parameters.description: Boolean. Specifies whether the notifier is enabled or not. A value of: true indicates that the notifier is enabled. false indicates that the notifier is disabled. If a notifier is disabled, alerts are not fetched for the notifier.

output_parameters.name: consumption​Mode
output_parameters.description: String. Specifies whether notifications are created for all alerts or only the latest alerts. A value of: earliest indicates that the notifications are generated for all alerts in the system. latest indicates that the notifications are generated only for the alerts emitted after the notifier is created.

output_parameters.name: autoMark
output_parameters.description: Boolean. Specifies whether notifications should be automatically marked as read or a user must do it manually. A value of: true indicates that the system automatically processes the notification. For example, a notification is automatically marked as read after the service specified in the notifier settings is invoked. false indicates that a user must manually mark the notification as read.

output_parameters.name: channelIds
output_parameters.description: Object[]. List of identifiers of the channels for which you want to receive notifications.

output_parameters.name: interval​InSeconds
output_parameters.description: Short. Time in seconds after which the notifier should check for unread alerts.

output_parameters.name: severityCutoff
output_parameters.description: String. The severity to consider for fetching alerts. For example, if this value is Critical, you will receive notifications only for critical events. If this value is Error, you will receive notifications for critical and error events. This parameter can have one of the following values: Critical Error Information Warning

output_parameters.name: notifyInstantly
output_parameters.description: Boolean. Specifies whether or not notifications is generated as soon as an alert is raised. A value of: true indicates that notifications are generated instantly for an alert. false indicates that notifications are not generated instantly for an alert. Note: If intervalInSeconds is also specified, the notifyInstantly parameter takes precedence.

output_parameters.name: webSocket​Enabled
output_parameters.description: Boolean. Specifies whether or not Integration Server uses a WebSocket connection to send notifications. A value of : true indicates that a WebSocket connection is enabled to send notifications. false indicates that a WebSocket connection is disabled.

output_parameters.name: webSocket​Sender
output_parameters.description: Document. Sends a message on a connected WebSocket session.

output_parameters.name: endPointURL
output_parameters.description: String. The URL of the remote WebSocket server that Integration Server uses to send notifications.

output_parameters.name: service​InvokeEnabled
output_parameters.description: Boolean. Specifies whether a service is invoked with the notifier parameters as pipeline variables or not. A value of: true indicates that a service can be invoked using the notifier parameters. false indicates that a service invocation is disabled for the notifier.

output_parameters.name: service​InvokeSender
output_parameters.description: Document. Invokes a service for alerts fetched for this notifier.

output_parameters.name: nsName
output_parameters.description: String. The namespace of the service. For example, pub.flow:tracePipeline

output_parameters.name: smtpEnabled
output_parameters.description: Boolean. Specifies whether Integration Server uses an SMTP server to send notifications or not.

output_parameters.name: smtpSender
output_parameters.description: Document. Details of the SMTP Server and client used to send the notifications.

output_parameters.name: to
output_parameters.description: String. E-mail address of the recipient for a notification. Multiple addresses are separated with commas.

output_parameters.name: from
output_parameters.description: String. E-mail address of the sender. If you do not specify this value, Integration Server uses the value specified for the mail.smtp.from JVM property. Furthermore, if a value is not specified for that property, Integration Server uses the default value, user@servername, where the user is the operating system user ID, and servername is the host name of Integration Server.

output_parameters.name: mailhost
output_parameters.description: String. Hostname of the SMTP server.

output_parameters.name: mailhostPort
output_parameters.description: Integer. The port on which the SMTP server listens to incoming connections.

output_parameters.name: auth
output_parameters.description: Document. Authorization information that the SMTP service submits to the SMTP server.

output_parameters.name: user
output_parameters.description: String. Username that Integration Server uses to send notifications.

output_parameters.name: secure
output_parameters.description: Document. Parameters specifying the security protocol and trust store information to perform certificate validation, which Integration Server uses to communicate with the SMTP server port.

output_parameters.name: transport​Layer​Security
output_parameters.description: String. Type of the security protocol that Integration Server uses to communicate with the SMTP server port. Set to: none, to use a non-secure mode to communicate with the port on the SMTP server. This is the default. explicit, to use explicit security when communicating with the port on the SMTP server. With explicit security, Integration Server establishes an un-encrypted connection to the e-mail server, and then switches to the secure mode. implicit, to use implicit security to communicate with the port on the SMTP server. With implicit security, Integration Server always establishes an encrypted connection to the e-mail server.

output_parameters.name: truststore​Alias
output_parameters.description: String. Alias for the truststore that contains the list of certificates that Integration Server uses to validate the trust relationship. If you do not specify a truststore alias, the default truststore alias is used.

usage_notes: Prior to Integration Server version 10.15, the pub.alert.notifier:list service included the smtpSender/secure/useJSSE output parameter which specified whether Integration Server created the outbound connection using the Java Secure Socket Extension (JSSE) library. Beginning with Integration Serverversion 10.15, Integration Server establishes all secure outbound connections with JSSE. If you migrated to Integration Server 10.15 and a migrated service invokes pub.alert.notifier:list, the smtpSender/secure/useJSSE parameter still appears in the pipeline. However, Integration Server ignores the value of the smtpSender/secure/useJSSE parameter, proceeding as if smtpSender/secure/useJSSE were set to yes.

services.name: pub.alert.notifier:update
services.description: WmPublic. Updates the parameters of an existing notifier.

input_parameters.name: name
input_parameters.description: String. Name of the notifier.

input_parameters.name: enabled
input_parameters.description: Boolean. Optional. Specifies whether the notifier is enabled or not. Set to: true to enable the notifier. false to disable the notifier. This is the default. If a notifier is disabled, alerts are not fetched for the notifier.

input_parameters.name: consumptionMode
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. Specifies whether notifications are created for all alerts or only the latest alerts. Set to: earliest to get notifications for all alerts in the system. This is the default. latest to get notifications only for the alerts emitted after the notifier is created.

input_parameters.name: autoMark
input_parameters.description: Boolean. Optional. Specifies whether notifications are automatically marked as read or a user must do it manually. Set to: true to automatically mark a notification as read after the service specified in the notifier settings is invoked. This is the default. false to manually dismiss a notification from Integration Server Administrator, which marks the notification as read.

input_parameters.name: channelIds
input_parameters.description: Object[]. Identifies the channels for which you want to receive notifications. You can specify either an individual channel identifier or a list of identifiers. For example, channelIds[0] as 2 for "Server Error" or channelIds[1] as 10 for "Threat Protection" or channelIds[0] as 2 and channelIds[1] as 1 for both.

input_parameters.name: intervalInSeconds
input_parameters.description: Short. Time in seconds after which the system task created for the notifier should check for unread alerts.

input_parameters.name: severityCutoff
input_parameters.description: String. The severity to consider for fetching alerts. For example, if you set this value as Critical, you will receive notifications only for critical events. If you set this value as Error, you will receive notifications for critical and error events. Set the parameter to one of the following values: Critical. This is the default. Error Information Warning

input_parameters.name: notifyInstantly
input_parameters.description: Boolean. Optional. Specifies whether or not notifications are generated as soon as an alert is raised. Set to: true to instantly generate a notification for an alert. false if an instant notification is not required for an alert. This is the default. Note: If intervalInSeconds is also specified, the notifyInstantly parameter takes precedence.

input_parameters.name: webSocketEnabled
input_parameters.description: Boolean. Optional. Specifies whether or not Integration Server uses a WebSocket connection to send notifications. Set to: true to enable a WebSocket connection to send notifications. false to disable a WebSocket connection. This is the default.

input_parameters.name: webSocketSender
input_parameters.description: Document. Optional. Sends a message on a connected WebSocket session.

input_parameters.name: endPointURL
input_parameters.description: String. The URL of the remote WebSocket server that Integration Server uses to send notifications.

input_parameters.name: serviceInvokeEnabled
input_parameters.description: Boolean. Optional. Specifies whether a service is invoked with the notifier parameters as pipeline variables or not. Set to: true to invoke a service using the notifier parameters. false to disable service invocation for the notifier. This is the default.

input_parameters.name: serviceInvokeSender
input_parameters.description: Document. Optional. Invokes a service for alerts fetched for this notifier.

input_parameters.name: nsName
input_parameters.description: String. The namespace of the service. For example, pub.flow:tracePipeline .

input_parameters.name: smtpEnabled
input_parameters.description: Boolean. Optional. Specifies whether Integration Server uses an SMTP server to send notifications or not. Set to: true to use an SMTP server to send notifications. false if you do not want to use an SMTP server to send notifications. This is the default.

input_parameters.name: smtpSender
input_parameters.description: Document. Optional. Details of the SMTP Server and client used to send the notifications.

input_parameters.name: to
input_parameters.description: String. E-mail address of the recipient for a notification. If you specify multiple addresses, separate them with commas.

input_parameters.name: from
input_parameters.description: String. E-mail address of the sender. If you do not specify this value, Integration Server uses the value specified for the mail.smtp.from JVM property. Furthermore, if a value is not specified for that property, Integration Server uses the default value, user@servername, where the user is the operating system user ID, and servername is the host name of Integration Server.

input_parameters.name: mailhost
input_parameters.description: String. Hostname of the SMTP server.

input_parameters.name: mailhostPort
input_parameters.description: Integer. Optional. The port on which the SMTP server listens to incoming connections.

input_parameters.name: auth
input_parameters.description: Document. Optional. Authorization information that the SMTP service submits to the SMTP server.

input_parameters.name: user
input_parameters.description: String. Username that Integration Server uses to send notifications.

input_parameters.name: pass
input_parameters.description: String. Password associated with the user.

input_parameters.name: secure
input_parameters.description: Document. Parameters specifying the security protocol and trust store information to perform certificate validation, which Integration Server uses to communicate with the SMTP server port.

input_parameters.name: transport​Layer​Security
input_parameters.description: String. Type of the security protocol that Integration Server uses to communicate with the SMTP server port. Set to: none, to use a non-secure mode to communicate with the port on the SMTP server. This is the default. explicit, to use explicit security when communicating with the port on the SMTP server. With explicit security, Integration Server establishes an un-encrypted connection to the e-mail server, and then switches to the secure mode. implicit, to use implicit security to communicate with the port on the SMTP server. With implicit security, Integration Server always establishes an encrypted connection to the e-mail server.

input_parameters.name: truststore​Alias
input_parameters.description: String. Alias for the truststore that contains the list of certificates that Integration Server uses to validate the trust relationship. If you do not specify a truststore alias, the default truststore alias is used.

output_parameters.name: message
output_parameters.description: String. Indicates whether a notifier is successfully updated or not.

usage_notes: When a notifier is enabled, if consumptionMode is changed from earliest to latest, all alerts generated until then are marked as read.

Prior to Integration Server version 10.15, the pub.alert.notifier:update service included the smtpSender/secure/useJSSE input parameter which specified whether Integration Server created the outbound connection using the Java Secure Socket Extension (JSSE) library. Beginning with Integration Serverversion 10.15, Integration Server establishes all secure outbound connections with JSSE. If you migrated to Integration Server 10.15 and a migrated service invokes pub.alert.notifier:update, the smtpSender/secure/useJSSE parameter still appears in the pipeline. However, Integration Server ignores the value of the smtpSender/secure/useJSSE parameter, proceeding as if useJSSE were set to yes.

services.name: pub.alert:channels
services.description: WmPublic. Gets all alert channels in the system. Channels help classify alerts and lets you subscribe to a specific type of alerts.

output_parameters.name: Channels
output_parameters.description: Document List. The list of alert channels in Integration Server. By default, there are 13 channels. All channels have the following keys:

output_parameters.name: id
output_parameters.description: Integer. Identifier of a channel. This is a system assigned value and cannot be modified. Note: Channel IDs for custom channels start with 1001.

output_parameters.name: displayName
output_parameters.description: String. Name of the alert channel.

output_parameters.name: description
output_parameters.description: String. Description for the channel.

output_parameters.name: emissionEnabled
output_parameters.description: Boolean. Indicates whether an alert is generated for the channel and stored in the database or not. A value of: true indicates that alerts are emitted for the channel and stored in the database. false indicates that alerts are disabled for the channel.

output_parameters.name: systemNotificationEnabled
output_parameters.description: Boolean. Indicates whether an alert generated for the channel is displayed as a notification or not. true indicates that notifications are generated for the channel. false indicates that notifications are disabled for the channel.

usage_notes: Package subscription (Delivery)
Package subscription (Release)

services.name: pub.alert:countAll
services.description: WmPublic. Gets the count of all alerts in the system, read and unread, or a subset of alerts that meet the filter criteria.

input_parameters.name: filter
input_parameters.description: Document. Optional. To get the count of a subset of alerts, specify the criteria using the following keys:

input_parameters.name: startTime
input_parameters.description: Long. Optional. Start of the time interval in which alerts are emitted. Specify the value in UNIX epoch format in milliseconds.

input_parameters.name: endTime
input_parameters.description: Long. Optional. End of the time interval in which alerts are emitted. Specify the value in UNIX epoch format in milliseconds.

input_parameters.name: severities
input_parameters.description: String[]. Optional. List of severities.

input_parameters.name: channelIds
input_parameters.description: Object[]. Optional. List of channel identifiers.

input_parameters.name: user
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. The Integration Server user who triggered the alerts or Administrator for alerts emitted by the server. For example, specify Administrator for Password Management alerts.

input_parameters.name: keyword
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. A keyword that identifies the alerts.

output_parameters.name: count
output_parameters.description: Long. The count of all alerts in the system, read and unread, or a subset of alerts, if the filter criteria are passed as inputs.

usage_notes: To get the count of all alerts in the system, read and unread, do not specify the filter criteria as inputs.

services.name: pub.alert:countUnread
services.description: WmPublic. Gets the count of all unread alerts in the system or a subset of unread alerts for the notifier and filter criteria.

input_parameters.name: notifierName
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. Name of the notifier for which alerts are to be fetched. If you do not specify the notifier name, all unread alerts in the system or a subset of the unread alerts that meet the filter criteria are considered.

input_parameters.name: filter
input_parameters.description: Document. Optional. To get the count of a subset of unread alerts, specify the criteria using the following keys:

input_parameters.name: startTime
input_parameters.description: Long. Optional. Start of the time interval in which alerts are emitted. Specify the value in UNIX epoch format in milliseconds.

input_parameters.name: endTime
input_parameters.description: Long. Optional. End of the time interval in which alerts are emitted. Specify the value in UNIX epoch format in milliseconds.

input_parameters.name: severities
input_parameters.description: String[]. Optional. List of severities.

input_parameters.name: channelIds
input_parameters.description: Object[]. Optional. List of channel identifiers.

input_parameters.name: user
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. The Integration Server user who triggered the alerts or Administrator for alerts emitted by the server. For example, specify Administrator for Password Management alerts.

input_parameters.name: keyword
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. A keyword that identifies the alerts.

output_parameters.name: count
output_parameters.description: Long. The count of all unread alerts in the system or a subset of unread alerts, if the notifier name and the filter criteria are specified.

usage_notes: Integration Server uses the pub.alert:countUnread service to display the count of unread notifications on the Notifications icon in Integration Server Administrator.
When notifierName is specified in the pub.alert:countUnread service, the severities and channelIds parameters under filter are evaluated in the following manner:
Consider a notifier with the following settings and the notifierName is passed to the pub.alert:countUnread service.

services.name: pub.alert:deregisterChannel
services.description: WmPublic. Deregisters a registered custom channel.

input_parameters.name: channelId
input_parameters.description: Integer. Identifier of the channel that you want to deregister.

output_parameters.name: deregistered
output_parameters.description: Boolean. Indicates whether the channel is deregistered or not. A value of: true indicates that the channel is deregistered. false indicates that the channel is not deregistered.

usage_notes: Channel IDs for custom channels are generated by the system and cannot be modified. The first custom channel gets 1001 as the channel ID and subsequent custom channels have consecutive numbers. Once a custom channel is deregistered, the channel ID is no longer available for a new custom channel until Integration Server is restarted.
Deregistering a channel also deletes all the alerts for that channel.

services.name: pub.alert:emit
services.description: WmPublic. Generates an alert when a pre-configured condition is met. For example, User or Master Password Expiry, License Key Update, Enterprise Gateway Rule Violation, Service Error, and so on. This service is invoked by Integration Server components to raise alerts.

input_parameters.name: channelID
input_parameters.description: Integer. Identifier of the channel associated with the alert.

input_parameters.name: severity
input_parameters.description: String. Severity associated with the alert channel.

input_parameters.name: subject
input_parameters.description: String. Subject of the alert message.

input_parameters.name: contentType
input_parameters.description: String. Type of information in the alert. For example, text/plain.

input_parameters.name: content

output_parameters.name: alertId
output_parameters.description: Long. A unique identifier assigned to each alert emitted.

services.name: pub.alert:fetchAll
services.description: WmPublic. Fetches all alerts in the system, read and unread, or a subset of alerts that meet the filter criteria.

input_parameters.name: filter
input_parameters.description: Document. Optional. To fetch a subset of alerts, specify the criteria using the following keys:

input_parameters.name: startTime
input_parameters.description: Long. Optional. Start of the time interval in which alerts are emitted. Specify the value in the UNIX epoch format in milliseconds.

input_parameters.name: endTime
input_parameters.description: Long. Optional. End of the time interval in which alerts are emitted. Specify the value in the UNIX epoch format in milliseconds.

input_parameters.name: severities
input_parameters.description: String[]. Optional. List of severities.

input_parameters.name: channelIds
input_parameters.description: Object[]. Optional. List of channel identifiers.

input_parameters.name: user
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. The Integration Server user who triggered the alerts or Administrator for alerts emitted by the server. For example, specify Administrator for Password Management alerts.

input_parameters.name: keyword
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. A keyword that identifies the alerts.

input_parameters.name: sortOrder
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. The order in which the fetched alerts are sorted. Select ASC or DESC. The default is DESC.

input_parameters.name: sortBy
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. The criteria to sort the fetched alerts. Select one of the following options: channel severity subject timestamp The default is timestamp.

input_parameters.name: pageSize
input_parameters.description: Integer. The number of notifications on a page.

input_parameters.name: pageNumber
input_parameters.description: Integer. In a paginated list of notifications, the page where Integration Server loads the notifications. Note: Page number starts with 0.

output_parameters.name: alerts
output_parameters.description: Document List. A list of the alerts retrieved by the service. Each alert has the following details:

output_parameters.name: id
output_parameters.description: Long. Alert identifier.

output_parameters.name: ts
output_parameters.description: Long. Date and time when the alert is generated, in the UNIX epoch time format.

output_parameters.name: serverId
output_parameters.description: String. IP address of the host where Integration Server is running.

output_parameters.name: memberName
output_parameters.description: String. Member name of the machine that hosts Integration Server in a cluster.

output_parameters.name: user
output_parameters.description: String. Integration Server user who triggered the alert.

output_parameters.name: channelId
output_parameters.description: Integer. Channel identifier for the alert.

output_parameters.name: channel
output_parameters.description: String. Channel for the alert.

output_parameters.name: severityId
output_parameters.description: Integer. Severity identifier of the alert.

output_parameters.name: severity
output_parameters.description: String. Severity of the alert.

output_parameters.name: subject
output_parameters.description: String. Subject of the alert message.

output_parameters.name: contentType
output_parameters.description: String. Text that identifies the type of content in the alert such as text/plain and text/html.

output_parameters.name: content
output_parameters.description: String. Content of the alert.

usage_notes: To fetch all read and unread alerts in the system, do not specify the filter criteria as inputs.
The number of notifications on a page is identified by pageSize and the current page is identified by pageNumber.

services.name: pub.alert:fetchUnread
services.description: WmPublic. Fetches all the unread alerts in the system or a subset of unread alerts for the notifier and filter criteria.

input_parameters.name: notifierName
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. If you do not specify the notifier name, all the unread alerts in the system or a subset of the unread alerts that meet the filter criteria are fetched.

input_parameters.name: filter
input_parameters.description: Document. Optional. To fetch a subset of alerts, specify the criteria using the following keys:

input_parameters.name: startTime
input_parameters.description: Long. Optional. Start of the time interval in which alerts are emitted. Specify the value in the UNIX epoch format in milliseconds.

input_parameters.name: endTime
input_parameters.description: Long. Optional. End of the time interval in which alerts are emitted. Specify the value in the UNIX epoch format in milliseconds.

input_parameters.name: severities
input_parameters.description: String[]. Optional. List of severities.

input_parameters.name: channelIds
input_parameters.description: Object[]. Optional. List of channel identifiers.

input_parameters.name: user
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. The Integration Server user who triggered the alerts or Administrator for alerts emitted by the server. For example, specify Administrator for Password Management alerts.

input_parameters.name: keyword
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. A keyword that identifies the alerts.

input_parameters.name: sortOrder
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. The order in which the fetched alerts are sorted. Select ASC or DESC. The default is DESC.

input_parameters.name: sortBy
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. The criteria to sort the fetched alerts. Select one of the following options: channel severity subject timestamp The default is timestamp.

input_parameters.name: pageSize
input_parameters.description: Integer. The number of notifications on a page, identified by pageNumber

input_parameters.name: pageNumber
input_parameters.description: Integer. In a paginated list of notifications, the page where Integration Server loads the notifications. Note: Page number starts with 0.

output_parameters.name: alerts
output_parameters.description: Document List. A list of the alerts fetched by the service. Each alert has the following details:

output_parameters.name: id
output_parameters.description: Long. Alert identifier.

output_parameters.name: ts
output_parameters.description: Long. Date and time when the alert is generated, in the UNIX epoch time format.

output_parameters.name: serverId
output_parameters.description: String. IP address of the host where Integration Server is running.

output_parameters.name: memberName
output_parameters.description: String. Member name of the machine that hosts Integration Server in a cluster.

output_parameters.name: user
output_parameters.description: String. Integration Server user who triggered the alert.

output_parameters.name: channelId
output_parameters.description: Integer. Channel identifier for the alert.

output_parameters.name: channel
output_parameters.description: String. Channel for the alert.

output_parameters.name: severityId
output_parameters.description: Integer. Severity identifier of the alert.

output_parameters.name: severity
output_parameters.description: String. Severity of the alert.

output_parameters.name: subject
output_parameters.description: String. Subject of the alert message.

output_parameters.name: contentType
output_parameters.description: String. Text that identifies the type of content in the alert such as text/plain and text/html.

output_parameters.name: content
output_parameters.description: String. Content of the alert.

usage_notes: Integration Server uses the pub.alert:fetchUnread service to display the unread notifications in Integration Server Administrator.
When notifierName is specified in the pub.alert:fetchUnread service, the severities and channelIds parameters under filter are evaluated in the following manner:
Consider a notifier with the following settings and the notifierName is passed to the pub.alert:fetchUnread service.

The number of notifications on a page is identified by pageSize and the current page is identified by pageNumber. If pageSize is 10 and pageNumber is 0, with sortBy and sortOrder having default values, the latest 10 notifications are listed on the first page.

services.name: pub.alert:getChannel
services.description: WmPublic. Gets the details of a specific channel.

input_parameters.name: channelId
input_parameters.description: Integer. Identifier of the channel that you want to retrieve.

output_parameters.name: displayName
output_parameters.description: String. Name of the channel.

output_parameters.name: description
output_parameters.description: String. Description for the channel.

output_parameters.name: emissionEnabled
output_parameters.description: Boolean. Indicates whether an alert is generated for the channel and stored in the database or not. A value of: true indicates that alerts are emitted for the channel and stored in the database. false indicates that alerts are disabled for the channel.

output_parameters.name: systemNotificationEnabled
output_parameters.description: Boolean. Indicates whether an alert generated for the channel is displayed as a notification or not. true indicates that notifications are generated for the channel. false indicates that notifications are disabled for the channel.

services.name: pub.alert:getSettings
services.description: WmPublic. Gets the settings of the system notifier.

output_parameters.name: enabled​Emission​Channels
output_parameters.description: Object[]. List of channels for which alerts can be generated and stored in the database.

output_parameters.name: enabledSystem​NotifierChannels
output_parameters.description: Object[]. List of channels for which alerts are generated and displayed as notifications.

output_parameters.name: system​Notifier​​Severity​Cutoff
output_parameters.description: String. Notifications are generated only for alerts with severity equal to, or more than this value.

output_parameters.name: retentionPeriod​InDays
output_parameters.description: Integer. Determines how long the alerts are stored in the database.

output_parameters.name: certificate​Expiry​​Warning​InDays
output_parameters.description: Integer. Number of days before the certificate expiry date, when the alert should be generated.

services.name: pub.alert:markAllAsRead
services.description: WmPublic. Marks all alert messages as read.

input_parameters.name: notifierName
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. Name of the notifier for which all the alerts are to be marked as read. If you do not specify the notifier name, alerts of system channels are marked as read.

output_parameters.name: updateCount
output_parameters.description: Long. Updated count of the unread alerts in the system.

usage_notes: Invoke pub.alert:markAllAsRead to dismiss all notifications at once.

services.name: pub.alert:markAsRead
services.description: WmPublic. Marks the specified message as read.

input_parameters.name: alertId
input_parameters.description: Long. Specifies the alert that should be marked as read.

input_parameters.name: notifierName
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. Name of the notifier for which for which the alert should be marked as read.

output_parameters.name: updateCount
output_parameters.description: Long. Updated count of the unread alerts in the system.

usage_notes: Integration Server invokes pub.alert:markAsRead to dismiss a specific notification.

services.name: pub.alert:markAsUnread
services.description: WmPublic. Marks a specific alert as unread.

input_parameters.name: alertId
input_parameters.description: Long. The alert that you want to mark as unread.

input_parameters.name: notifierName
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. The notifier for which the alert should be marked as unread.

output_parameters.name: updateCount
output_parameters.description: Long. Updated count of the unread alerts in the system.

services.name: pub.alert:registerChannel
services.description: WmPublic. Registers a custom channel.

input_parameters.name: displayName
input_parameters.description: String. Name of the channel that you want to register.

input_parameters.name: description
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. Description of the channel.

input_parameters.name: emissionEnabled
input_parameters.description: Boolean. Optional. Indicates whether an alert is generated for the channel and stored in the database or not. Set to: true to emit alerts for the channel and store them in the database. false to disable alerts for the channel. The default value is true for custom channels as well as system channels other than Service Error and Server Error.

input_parameters.name: systemNotificationEnabled
input_parameters.description: Boolean. Optional. Indicates whether the alert generated for the channel is displayed as a notification or not. Set to: true to generate notifications for the channel. false to disable notifications for the channel. The default value is true for custom channels as well as system channels other than Service Error and Server Error.

output_parameters.name: channelId
output_parameters.description: Integer. Identifier of the registered channel. Note: Channel IDs for custom channels start with 1001.

usage_notes: For custom channels, channel IDs are generated by the system and cannot be modified. The first custom channel gets 1001 as the channel ID and subsequent custom channels have consecutive numbers. Once a custom channel is deregistered, the channel ID is no longer available for a new custom channel until Integration Server is restarted.

services.name: pub.alert:setSettings
services.description: WmPublic. Updates the settings of the system notifier.

input_parameters.name: enabled​Emission​Channels
input_parameters.description: Object[]. List of channels for which alerts can be generated and stored in the database.

input_parameters.name: enabledSystem​NotifierChannels
input_parameters.description: Object[]. List of channels for which alerts are generated and displayed as notifications.

input_parameters.name: systemNotifier​​SeverityCutoff
input_parameters.description: String. Notifications are generated only for alerts with severity equal to, or more than this value.

input_parameters.name: retentionPeriod​​InDays
input_parameters.description: Integer. Determines how long the alerts are stored in the database.

input_parameters.name: certificate​Expiry​​WarningInDays
input_parameters.description: Integer. Number of days before the certificate expiry date when the alert should be generated.

output_parameters.name: message
output_parameters.description: String. Indicates whether the system notifier settings are updated or not.

usage_notes: Based on the value of the retentionPeriodInDays parameter, a purge job clears the alerts stored in the database.

services.name: pub.alert:severities
services.description: WmPublic. Lists the pre-defined severities that you can associate with an alert channel. The severity associated with an alert lets you assess the relative impact of a business event on the system.

output_parameters.name: Severities
output_parameters.description: Document List. The list of default severities available in the system. By default, 4 severities are configured.

output_parameters.name: id
output_parameters.description: Integer. Identifier for a severity. The default severities and their IDs are as follows: 0: Critical, 1: Error, 2: Warning, 3: Information

output_parameters.name: displayName
output_parameters.description: String. The severity name.

services.name: pub.alert:updateChannel
services.description: The default value is true for custom channels as well as system channels other than Service Error and Server Error.

input_parameters.name: channelId
input_parameters.description: Integer. Identifier of the alert channel that you want to update. Note: Channel IDs for custom channels start with 1001.

input_parameters.name: description
input_parameters.description: String. Optional. Description of the channel.

input_parameters.name: emissionEnabled
input_parameters.description: Boolean. Optional. Indicates whether an alert is generated for the channel and stored in the database or not. Set to: true to emit alerts for the channel and store them in the database. false to disable alerts for the channel. The default value is true for custom channels as well as system channels other than Service Error and Server Error.

input_parameters.name: systemNotificationEnabled
input_parameters.description: Boolean. Optional. Indicates whether the alert generated for the channel is displayed as a notification or not. Set to: true to generate notifications for the channel. false to disable notifications for the channel. The default value is true for custom channels as well as system channels other than Service Error and Server Error.

output_parameters.name: message
output_parameters.description: String. Indicates whether the channel is successfully updated or not.

usage_notes: Invoke the pub.alert:updateChannel service to ignore a notification of a specific type or restore an ignored notification from Integration Server Administrator.
